Off Calendar Aug-10-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Janet Tapia, In Propria Persona (415-823-5373) appears self-represented plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Peter F. Finn of the law offices of Bradley Curley Barrabee & Kowalski (415-464-8888 x 275) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JANET TAPIA'S Motion For A New Trial

DENIED. The court lacks jurisdiction to grant a new trial. First, Tapia failed to file her notice of intention to move for a new trial in a timely fashion. The notice of entry of judgment was mail-served on May 17, 2018 and Tapia filed her notice of intention on July 5, 2018. This filing was outside the jurisdictional 15-day time period of CCP 659(a)(2). (See In re Marriage of Beilock (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 713, 721.) In addition, the court lacks jurisdiction to grant a new trial because the 60-day period to rule ran on July 16, 2018. (See CCP 660.) Written order is signed and filed.

RULING: There being no appearance by plaintiff, and no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF JANET TAPIA'S Motion To Quash Improper Subpoena And For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Counsel Peter Finn. The motion to quash subpoena is DENIED. Plaintiff has placed her mental health in issue in the lawsuit she brought - through discovery responses, deposition testimony and a letter from a mental health care provider. (See Britt v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.) The Court notes defendants' willingness to negotiate the subpoena's scope. No sanctions are awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.

Motion To Quash Improper Subpoena And For Sanctions Against Defts And Their Counsel Peter Finn
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Continued To Nov-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Janet Tapia, In Propria Persona (510-823-5373) appears as self-represented plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Peter F. Finn of the law offices of Bradley Curley Barrabee & Kowalski (415-464-8888) appears as counsel for defendants/opposing parties

Plaintiff declines to stipulate to judge pro tem. The cause is argued by both sides. Plaintiff exhibits some inability to focus on the Court's comments and questions. Plaintiff states she does not trust the court. Judge Pro Tem Stein explains and attempts to help Ms. Tapia understand and trust the process. Judge Pro Tem Stein offers Ms. Tapia three procedural alternatives and passes the matter for ten minutes to allow Ms. Tapia to consider. The alternatives are as follows:

1) Plaintiff may choose to withdraw her claim of "extreme emotional distress" from her first amended complaint, stipulate to judge pro tem and the court would grant her motion to quash in its entirety;
2) Ms. Tapia may stipulate to judge pro tem, lodge confidentially the subpoena records for the Court's review; if the records show nothing pertaining to claims of significant or severe mental/emotional distress plaintiff's motion would be granted in its entirety, if records indicate information relevant to significant or severe mental/emotional distress, the court would continue the matter, partially deny the motion and order only relevant records produced;
3) Plaintiff may decline stipulation and alternatives 1 and 2, in which the matter would be continued accordingly before Judge Kahn in the Law and Motion department.

The matter is re-called. Defendant counsel indicates he agrees, in principle, with the court's alternatives. There follows brief argument as to scope. Plaintiff inquires as to authority to produce subpoena records. The Court explains again. Plaintiff rejects alternatives 1 and 2, selecting alternative 3.

PLAINTIFF JANET TAPIA'S Motion To Quash Improper Subpoena And For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Counsel Peter Finn is CONTINUED to November 21, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter F. Finn, of Bradley Curley Barrabee & Kowalski, Tel. 415.464.8888 ext. 275;
No other appearances.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR SERVICES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action and overruled as to the first and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff Janet Tapia's claim for violation of the Unruh Civil Rights Act may not be time-barred. Ms. Tapia argues the statute of limitations for claims arising under the Unruh Civil Rights Act is unclear, and Bayview Senior Services has not shown why a two-year limitations period, as opposed to the three-year limitation period, should apply here. (See Semler v. General Elec. Capital Corp. (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1380, 1387.) The second and third causes of action for negligence and sexual battery are time-barred per CCP 335.1. The fourth cause of action for violation of Government Code 11135 is not time-barred pursuant to the three-year limitations period of CCP 388. Ms. Tapia alleges the relevant acts took place "on or around October 23, 2013." (Complaint, 3:22.) "[W]here a complaint, challenged by general demurrer charging failure to comply with the applicable statute of limitations, does not on its face disclose that the action was necessarily barred, courts should overrule the demurrer and permit the defense to be raised by answer." (Childs v. State of California (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 155, 160-61.) Bayview Senior Services fails to specify the uncertain aspects of the complaint thereby defeating the demurrer based on grounds of uncertainty. (See Fenton v. Groveland Community Services Dist. (1982) 135 Cal.App.3d 797, 809.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
